acceptanceReviewed.evidence: The validation bundle now records machine-checkable HDR/color non-claims under evidenceBoundary.machineCheckableClaims.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: The existing sample color metadata is explicit.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: evidenceBoundary.sampleColorMetadata records yuv420p, tv range, and unknown colorSpace/colorTransfer/colorPrimaries from ffprobe metadata.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: The repo does not treat the sample as HDR or color-management evidence.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: bundle-manifest.json, video-path notes, and encode-sample notes all state that HDR handling, color-management behavior, wide-gamut preservation, and end-to-end color accuracy are not validated.

acceptanceReviewed.criterion: Future HDR/color validation is separated from this evidence bundle.
acceptanceReviewed.evidence: bundle-manifest.json and notes.md state that a future proof needs appropriate source material, tagged metadata, and HDR/color validation.
jq parsing, focused jq predicate, git diff --check, just lint-lines, qd gate --phase ci, and nix develop -c just verify passed.

realWorldValidation.evidence: This audit-fix verifies evidence boundaries and non-claims only. It does not implement or validate HDR or color-management support.

resolvedFindings.finding: m2-video-path-validation does not validate HDR or color-management behavior
resolvedFindings.resolution: Resolved by making the observed sample metadata and HDR/color non-claims machine-checkable and documenting future validation requirements.

residualRisks: HDR handling, color-management behavior, wide-gamut preservation, and end-to-end color accuracy remain future product validation work.
